text.title: go-error-handling-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-error-handling-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: fmt.Errorf("getting user: %v", err) — using %v instead of %w.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: errors.Is(wrapped, ErrNotFound) returns false. The entire sentinel-error machinery stops working.

failure-mode.description: var appErr AppError; errors.As(err, &appErr) — appErr is a value, not a pointer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: errors.As requires the target to be a non-nil pointer to either an error type or any type implementing error. Value types silently fail to match.

failure-mode.description: data, _ := json.Marshal(user) — ignoring errors with blank identifier.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: json.Marshal fails on channels, functions, and cyclic structures. Silently ignoring the error sends malformed JSON to the client.

failure-mode.description: if err != nil { log.Printf(...); return err } — logging then returning.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every intermediate layer that logs-and-returns produces a duplicate log line. Only the outermost handler should log.

failure-mode.description: panic(err) to handle expected failures like user not found.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Panics propagate up the call stack and may crash the entire server if not recovered. Use error returns for expected conditions.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These mean the client cancelled or the deadline passed. Retrying wastes resources. Check errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) before retry.
